title: Direct Talk
subTitle: Yasuda Junichi directed `A Samurai in Time', a playful film made on a shoestring budget that became a word-of-mouth hit. Yasuda, who is also a rice farmer, gives us the behind-the-scenes story.

title: Trails to Oishii Tokyo
subTitle: Sanma is a fish that shines like a sword. In Japan, it's popular and reasonably-priced at around a dollar per fish. We visit Kesennuma, a sanma port in Tohoku, to learn about this autumn treat.

title: NHK World Japan
subTitle: For a time during the Edo period, fireworks lit up the skies practically every night in the summer. It was a chance for women to dress up and let loose and grab the attention of male suitors.

subTitle: NHK World Japan

title: Seasoning the Seasons
subTitle: Explores the history of the Seto Inland Sea in western Japan, famous for its abundance of stones, and about the traditions stemming from its stones, including stone baths and a musical instrument called sanukite.

title: Magical Japanese
subTitle: Focusing on words related to yama (mountains). Mountains make up about 75 per cent of Japan's territory, and they have made their way into many common expressions in the Japanese language.

title: Easy Japanese
subTitle: To express an impression of something, one has to use any adjective that ends with i, like kawaii, followed by the name of the object, and desu ne.

subTitle: Ukrainian musician Kateryna talks about her motivation to support displaced people in Japan after Russia's invasion of her homeland.
